74	# stopwording belongs upstream.
75	_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
76	    {
77	        "the",
78	        "and",
79	        "for",
80	        "with",
81	        "from",
82	        "that",
83	        "this",
84	        "are",
85	        "was",
86	        "were",
87	        "has",
88	        "have",
89	        "had",
90	        "will",
91	        "would",
92	        "could",
93	        "should",
94	        "into",
95	        "onto",
96	        "over",
97	        "under",
98	        "than",
99	        "then",
100	        "but",
101	        "not",
102	        "any",
103	        "all",
104	        "some",
105	        "such",
106	        "their",
107	        "there",
108	        "where",
109	        "when",
110	        "what",
111	        "which",
112	        "while",
113	        "about",
114	        "between",
115	        "across",
116	        "after",
117	        "before",
118	        "because",
119	        "been",
120	        "being",
121	        "they",
122	        "them",
123	        "its",
124	        "his",
125	        "her",
126	        "our",
127	        "your",
128	    }
129	)
130	
131	
132	def _tokens(value: Any) -> set[str]:
133	    if value is None:
134	        return set()
135	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple, set, frozenset)):
136	        out: set[str] = set()
137	        for item in value:
138	            out |= _tokens(item)
139	        return out
140	    text = str(value)
141	    pieces = "".join(ch.lower() if ch.isalnum() else " " for ch in text).split()
142	    return {token for token in pieces if len(token) >= 3 and token not in _STOPWORDS}
143	
144	
145	def _jaccard(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
146	    if not left or not right:
147	        return 0.0
148	    return len(left & right) / len(left | right)
149	
150	
151	def _overlap_coefficient(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
152	    """Symmetric overlap normalized by the smaller set.
153	
154	    Jaccard punishes large token bags that share a meaningful core,
155	    which is the wrong signal when comparing case-token bags (rich on
156	    one side, sparse on the other). Overlap coefficient asks
157	    "how much of the smaller bag is shared?", which is closer to what
158	    structural similarity means here. Used for case-bag-against-bag
159	    comparison; precondition / mechanism comparisons keep using
160	    Jaccard since both sides are small and targeted.
161	    """
162	
163	    if not left or not right:
164	        return 0.0
165	    return len(left & right) / min(len(left), len(right))
166	
167	
168	def _clamp(value: float, low: float, high: float) -> float:
169	    return max(low, min(high, value))
170	
171	
172	def _confidence_to_scalar(band: Any) -> float:
173	    name = getattr(band, "value", band)
174	    if name == PrincipleConfidence.HIGH.value:
175	        return 1.0
176	    if name == PrincipleConfidence.MODERATE.value:
177	        return 0.65
178	    return 0.35
179	
180	
181	# ── Inputs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
182	
183	
184	@dataclass(frozen=True)
185	class TransferQuery:
186	    """Description of a new situation against which principles are tested.
187	
188	    Only ``case_id`` and a usable text (any of ``title``, ``mechanism``,
189	    ``outcome_question``, ``source_text``) are required. Empty fields
190	    are treated as missing, not falsified — the engine reports
191	    ``low_confidence`` rather than scoring against absent data.
192	    """
193	
194	    case_id: str
195	    title: str = ""
196	    domain: str = ""
197	    actors: tuple[str, ...] = ()
198	    institutions: tuple[str, ...] = ()
199	    mechanism: str = ""
200	    preconditions_present: tuple[str, ...] = ()
201	    preconditions_absent: tuple[str, ...] = ()
202	    failure_signals_present: tuple[str, ...] = ()
203	    outcome_question: str = ""
204	    time_horizon_days: int | None = None
205	    source_text: str = ""
206	    observed_at: datetime | None = None
207	
208	    def all_text(self) -> str:
209	        parts = [
210	            self.title,
211	            self.mechanism,
212	            self.outcome_question,
213	            self.source_text,
214	            " ".join(self.actors),
215	            " ".join(self.institutions),
216	            " ".join(self.preconditions_present),
217	            " ".join(self.failure_signals_present),
218	        ]
219	        return " ".join(p for p in parts if p)
220	
221	
222	@dataclass(frozen=True)
223	class TransferMetric:
224	    name: str
225	    value: float
226	    range_low: float
227	    range_high: float
228	    method: str
229	    low_confidence: bool = False
230	    detail: str = ""
231	
232	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
233	        return {
234	            "name": self.name,
235	            "value": round(float(self.value), 6),
236	            "range": [self.range_low, self.range_high],
237	            "method": self.method,
238	            "low_confidence": bool(self.low_confidence),
239	            "detail": self.detail,
240	        }
241	
242	
243	class TransferStance(str):
244	    """String constants for stance values.
245	
246	    Kept as plain strings (not :class:`Enum`) so a recommendation
247	    serializes byte-identically across processes without enum
248	    representation drift.
249	    """
250	
251	    APPLIES = "APPLIES"
252	    WATCH = "WATCH"
253	    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
254	    DOES_NOT_APPLY = "DOES_NOT_APPLY"
255	
256	
257	_STANCE_RANK = {
258	    TransferStance.DOES_NOT_APPLY: 0,
259	    TransferStance.ABSTAIN: 1,
260	    TransferStance.WATCH: 2,
261	    TransferStance.APPLIES: 3,
262	}
263	
264	
265	@dataclass(frozen=True)
266	class TransferRecommendation:
267	    principle_id: str
268	    canonical_statement: str
269	    stance: str
270	    confidence: float
271	    closest_case_ids: tuple[str, ...]
272	    metrics: tuple[TransferMetric, ...]
273	    reasons: tuple[str, ...]
274	
275	    def metric(self, name: str) -> TransferMetric | None:
276	        for m in self.metrics:
277	            if m.name == name:
278	                return m
279	        return None
280	
281	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
282	        return {
283	            "principle_id": self.principle_id,
284	            "canonical_statement": self.canonical_statement,
285	            "stance": self.stance,
286	            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
287	            "closest_case_ids": list(self.closest_case_ids),
288	            "metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.metrics],
289	            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
290	        }
291	
292	
293	@dataclass(frozen=True)
294	class TransferReport:
295	    query_case_id: str
296	    recommendations: tuple[TransferRecommendation, ...]
297	    summary_metrics: tuple[TransferMetric, ...]
298	    best_principle_id: str | None
299	    best_stance: str
300	    trace_version: str = TRANSFER_VERSION
301	
302	    def recommendation(self, principle_id: str) -> TransferRecommendation | None:
303	        for rec in self.recommendations:
304	            if rec.principle_id == principle_id:
305	                return rec
306	        return None
307	
308	    def summary(self, name: str) -> TransferMetric | None:
309	        for m in self.summary_metrics:
310	            if m.name == name:
311	                return m
312	        return None
313	
314	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
315	        return {
316	            "query_case_id": self.query_case_id,
317	            "best_principle_id": self.best_principle_id,
318	            "best_stance": self.best_stance,
319	            "recommendations": [r.to_dict() for r in self.recommendations],
320	            "summary_metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.summary_metrics],
321	            "trace_version": self.trace_version,
322	        }
323	
324	
325	# ── Per-pair scoring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	
327	
328	def _structural_fit(
329	    query: TransferQuery,
330	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
331	    *,
332	    mechanism_score: float,
333	) -> TransferMetric:
334	    """Composite of precondition coverage, mechanism match (computed
335	    upstream against supporting cases), and outcome alignment.
336	
337	    Preconditions and outcomes are scored with Jaccard against the
338	    principle's own targeted fields; mechanism is supplied by the
339	    caller so the same case-aware mechanism scoring used elsewhere
340	    in the report is what feeds the fit axis. This avoids penalizing
341	    a principle whose terse one-line ``mechanism`` text happens not
342	    to share vocabulary with the query when the supporting cases'
343	    mechanisms clearly do.
344	    """
345	
346	    query_text = _tokens(query.all_text())
347	    pre_terms = _tokens(principle.preconditions)
348	    out_terms = _tokens(principle.expected_outcomes)
349	
350	    pre_score = _jaccard(_tokens(query.preconditions_present), pre_terms) if pre_terms else 0.0
351	    if pre_terms and not query.preconditions_present:
352	        pre_score = max(pre_score, _jaccard(query_text, pre_terms) * 0.5)
353	    out_score = (
354	        _overlap_coefficient(_tokens(query.outcome_question) or query_text, out_terms)
355	        if out_terms
356	        else 0.0
357	    )
358	
359	    components = (pre_score, mechanism_score, out_score)
360	    if all(c == 0.0 for c in components):
361	        return TransferMetric(
362	            name="structural_fit",
363	            value=0.0,
364	            range_low=0.0,
365	            range_high=1.0,
366	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
367	            low_confidence=True,
368	            detail="no structural overlap on preconditions, mechanism, or outcome",
369	        )
370	
371	    weights = (0.45, 0.4, 0.15)
372	    weighted = sum(w * v for w, v in zip(weights, components))
373	    return TransferMetric(
374	        name="structural_fit",
375	        value=_clamp(weighted, 0.0, 1.0),
376	        range_low=0.0,
377	        range_high=1.0,
378	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
379	        detail=(
380	            f"pre={pre_score:.3f} mech={mechanism_score:.3f} outcome={out_score:.3f}"
381	        ),
382	    )
383	
384	
385	def _missing_preconditions(
386	    query: TransferQuery,
387	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
388	) -> TransferMetric:
389	    """Fraction of recorded preconditions whose tokens are *not* present
390	    in the query's stated preconditions or surrounding text.
391	    """
392	
393	    if not principle.preconditions:
394	        return TransferMetric(
395	            name="missing_preconditions",
396	            value=0.0,
397	            range_low=0.0,
398	            range_high=1.0,
399	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
400	            low_confidence=True,
401	            detail="principle has no stated preconditions",
402	        )
403	    haystack = _tokens(query.preconditions_present) | _tokens(query.all_text())
404	    missing = 0
405	    for pre in principle.preconditions:
406	        terms = _tokens(pre)
407	        if not terms:
408	            continue
409	        if not (terms & haystack):
410	            missing += 1
411	    fraction = missing / len(principle.preconditions)
412	    return TransferMetric(
413	        name="missing_preconditions",
414	        value=_clamp(fraction, 0.0, 1.0),
415	        range_low=0.0,
416	        range_high=1.0,
417	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
418	        detail=f"{missing}/{len(principle.preconditions)} preconditions unmatched",
419	    )
420	
421	
422	def _contradiction_risk(
423	    query: TransferQuery,
424	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
425	) -> TransferMetric:
426	    """Fraction of failure conditions whose detectable signal appears
427	    in the query, plus a boost when a negation candidate's tokens are
428	    densely present in the query text.
429	    """
430	
431	    fc = principle.failure_conditions
432	    nc = principle.negation_candidates
433	    if not fc and not nc:
434	        return TransferMetric(
435	            name="contradiction_risk",
436	            value=0.0,
437	            range_low=0.0,
438	            range_high=1.0,
439	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
440	            low_confidence=True,
441	            detail="principle has no failure_conditions or negation_candidates",
442	        )
443	
444	    # Baseline tokens describe the *principle itself*; they appear in
445	    # failure-condition descriptions only as context. We strip them so
446	    # the test asks "are the failure-specific tokens present?" rather
447	    # than "does the failure description share any vocabulary with
448	    # the query?".
449	    baseline = (
450	        _tokens(principle.preconditions)
451	        | _tokens(principle.mechanism)
452	        | _tokens(principle.expected_outcomes)
453	        | _tokens(principle.domain)
454	    )
455	    explicit_signals = _tokens(query.failure_signals_present) | _tokens(
456	        query.preconditions_absent
457	    )
458	    text_signals = _tokens(query.all_text())
459	    fc_hits = 0
460	    for cond in fc:
461	        signal_terms = _tokens(cond.detectable_signal) - baseline
462	        desc_terms = _tokens(cond.description) - baseline
463	        # An explicit failure signal is a strong contradiction: even
464	        # a single specific match counts. Inferring contradiction from
465	        # ambient text requires a denser overlap so a stray word in
466	        # a long passage doesn't trigger the veto.
467	        if signal_terms and (signal_terms & explicit_signals):
468	            fc_hits += 1
469	            continue
470	        novel = signal_terms | desc_terms
471	        if not novel:
472	            continue
473	        overlap = novel & text_signals
474	        if len(overlap) >= 2 or (overlap and len(novel) <= 3):
475	            fc_hits += 1
476	    fc_fraction = (fc_hits / len(fc)) if fc else 0.0
477	
478	    nc_score = 0.0
479	    if nc:
480	        nc_terms: set[str] = set()
481	        for n in nc:
482	            nc_terms |= _tokens(n.statement)
483	        nc_specific = nc_terms - baseline
484	        if nc_specific:
485	            nc_score = _jaccard(nc_specific, text_signals)
486	
487	    value = _clamp(max(fc_fraction, nc_score), 0.0, 1.0)
488	    return TransferMetric(
489	        name="contradiction_risk",
490	        value=value,
491	        range_low=0.0,
492	        range_high=1.0,
493	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
494	        detail=(
495	            f"failure_hits={fc_hits}/{len(fc) if fc else 0} "
496	            f"negation_jaccard={nc_score:.3f}"
497	        ),
498	    )
499	
500	
501	def _case_token_bag(case: EmpiricalCaseStudy) -> set[str]:
502	    return (
503	        _tokens(case.observed_mechanism)
504	        | _tokens(case.outcome)
505	        | _tokens(case.actors)
506	        | _tokens(case.institutions)
507	        | _tokens(case.stated_causal_claim)
508	        | _tokens(case.title)
509	        | _tokens(case.domain)
510	    )
511	
512	
513	def _case_distance_and_mechanism(
514	    query: TransferQuery,
515	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
516	    cases_by_id: Mapping[str, EmpiricalCaseStudy],
517	) -> tuple[TransferMetric, TransferMetric, tuple[str, ...]]:
518	    """Closest-case distance + mechanism match, plus the case ids that
519	    contributed to the closest scores.
520	
521	    Returns three things in one pass to avoid re-walking the supporting
522	    case list.
523	    """
524	
525	    supporting_ids = list(principle.supporting_case_ids)
526	    bounding_ids = list(principle.bounding_case_ids)
527	    contradicting_ids = list(principle.contradicting_case_ids)
528	    relevant_ids = supporting_ids + bounding_ids + contradicting_ids
529	
530	    query_bag = _tokens(query.all_text())
531	    query_mech = _tokens(query.mechanism) or query_bag
532	
533	    similarities: list[tuple[str, float, float]] = []  # (case_id, structural_sim, mech_sim)
534	    for cid in relevant_ids:
535	        case = cases_by_id.get(cid)
536	        if case is None:
537	            continue
538	        case_bag = _case_token_bag(case)
539	        structural = _overlap_coefficient(query_bag, case_bag)
540	        mech_sim = _overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(case.observed_mechanism))
541	        similarities.append((cid, structural, mech_sim))
542	
543	    if not similarities:
544	        distance = TransferMetric(
545	            name="case_distance",
546	            value=1.0,
547	            range_low=0.0,
548	            range_high=1.0,
549	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
550	            low_confidence=True,
551	            detail="no supporting / bounding / contradicting cases available",
552	        )
553	        mechanism = TransferMetric(
554	            name="mechanism_match",
555	            value=_overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(principle.mechanism)),
556	            range_low=0.0,
557	            range_high=1.0,
558	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
559	            low_confidence=True,
560	            detail="no case-level mechanism evidence; using principle.mechanism",
561	        )
562	        return distance, mechanism, ()
563	
564	    similarities.sort(key=lambda triple: triple[1], reverse=True)
565	    structural_scores = [s for _, s, _ in similarities]
566	    mech_scores = [m for _, _, m in similarities]
567	    closest_struct = structural_scores[0]
568	    median_struct = median(structural_scores)
569	    closest_ids = tuple(cid for cid, s, _ in similarities[: min(3, len(similarities))])
570	
571	    distance_value = _clamp(1.0 - ((closest_struct + median_struct) / 2.0), 0.0, 1.0)
572	    distance = TransferMetric(
573	        name="case_distance",
574	        value=distance_value,
575	        range_low=0.0,
576	        range_high=1.0,
577	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
578	        detail=f"closest={closest_struct:.3f} median={median_struct:.3f} n={len(similarities)}",
579	    )
580	
581	    mech_principle = _overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(principle.mechanism))
582	    mech_value = _clamp(max(max(mech_scores), mech_principle), 0.0, 1.0)
583	    mechanism = TransferMetric(
584	        name="mechanism_match",
585	        value=mech_value,
586	        range_low=0.0,
587	        range_high=1.0,
588	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
589	        detail=(
590	            f"max_case_mech={max(mech_scores):.3f} principle_mech={mech_principle:.3f}"
591	        ),
592	    )
593	    return distance, mechanism, closest_ids
594	
595	
596	def _domain_shift(
597	    query: TransferQuery,
598	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
599	) -> TransferMetric:
600	    """0 if the query domain matches the principle's domain or scope;
601	    1 if no overlap; intermediate if the principle has broad scope but
602	    no exact match.
603	    """
604	
605	    q_domain = (query.domain or "").strip().lower()
606	    p_domain = (principle.domain or "").strip().lower()
607	    scope_terms = {s.strip().lower() for s in principle.scope if s}
608	
609	    if not q_domain and not p_domain and not scope_terms:
610	        return TransferMetric(
611	            name="domain_shift",
612	            value=0.5,
613	            range_low=0.0,
614	            range_high=1.0,
615	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
616	            low_confidence=True,
617	            detail="neither query nor principle declares a domain",
618	        )
619	    if not q_domain:
620	        return TransferMetric(
621	            name="domain_shift",
622	            value=0.6,
623	            range_low=0.0,
624	            range_high=1.0,
625	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
626	            low_confidence=True,
627	            detail="query has no declared domain",
628	        )
629	
630	    if q_domain == p_domain or q_domain in scope_terms:
631	        return TransferMetric(
632	            name="domain_shift",
633	            value=0.0,
634	            range_low=0.0,
635	            range_high=1.0,
636	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
637	            detail=f"query domain {q_domain!r} matches principle scope",
638	        )
639	    if any(q_domain in s or s in q_domain for s in {p_domain} | scope_terms if s):
640	        return TransferMetric(
641	            name="domain_shift",
642	            value=0.4,
643	            range_low=0.0,
644	            range_high=1.0,
645	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
646	            detail=f"partial domain overlap with scope={sorted(scope_terms)}",
647	        )
648	    return TransferMetric(
649	        name="domain_shift",
650	        value=1.0,
651	        range_low=0.0,
652	        range_high=1.0,
653	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
654	        detail=(
655	            f"query domain {q_domain!r} not in principle.domain={p_domain!r} "
656	            f"or scope={sorted(scope_terms)}"
657	        ),
658	    )
659	
660	
661	def _time_decay_risk(
662	    query: TransferQuery,
663	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
664	    *,
665	    now: datetime,
666	    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None,
667	    reference_days: int,
668	) -> TransferMetric:
669	    """Older supporting cases → higher decay risk. ``case_dates`` is
670	    an optional caller-supplied map of case_id → observation date; when
671	    absent we fall back to the principle's status (a ``BOUNDED`` or
672	    ``CONTRADICTED`` principle implies the recorded scope has already
673	    drifted, which is itself a decay signal).
674	    """
675	
676	    supporting = list(principle.supporting_case_ids)
677	    if case_dates and supporting:
678	        ages = []
679	        for cid in supporting:
680	            date = case_dates.get(cid)
681	            if date is None:
682	                continue
683	            ages.append(max(0.0, (now - _as_utc(date)).total_seconds() / 86400.0))
684	        if ages:
685	            mean_age = sum(ages) / len(ages)
686	            value = _clamp(mean_age / max(1.0, float(reference_days)), 0.0, 1.0)
687	            return TransferMetric(
688	                name="time_decay_risk",
689	                value=value,
690	                range_low=0.0,
691	                range_high=1.0,
692	                method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
693	                detail=f"mean_supporting_age_days={mean_age:.1f} ref={reference_days}d",
694	            )
695	
696	    status = getattr(principle.status, "value", principle.status)
697	    if status == PrincipleStatus.CONTRADICTED.value:
698	        return TransferMetric(
699	            name="time_decay_risk",
700	            value=0.8,
701	            range_low=0.0,
702	            range_high=1.0,
703	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
704	            detail="principle status=CONTRADICTED",
705	        )
706	    if status == PrincipleStatus.BOUNDED.value:
707	        return TransferMetric(
708	            name="time_decay_risk",
709	            value=0.5,
710	            range_low=0.0,
711	            range_high=1.0,
712	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
713	            detail="principle status=BOUNDED — scope drift recorded",
714	        )
715	    return TransferMetric(
716	        name="time_decay_risk",
717	        value=0.3,
718	        range_low=0.0,
719	        range_high=1.0,
720	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
721	        low_confidence=True,
722	        detail="no case dates supplied; using neutral prior",
723	    )
724	
725	
726	def _outcome_uncertainty(
727	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
728	    contradiction: TransferMetric,
729	    missing_pre: TransferMetric,
730	) -> TransferMetric:
731	    """1 − (principle calibration × precondition coverage), bumped by
732	    contradiction risk. The calibration is the principle's recorded
733	    confidence band — it caps how confident *any* transfer recommendation
734	    can be even when the structural axes align.
735	    """
736	
737	    cal = principle.confidence
738	    cal_score = float(getattr(cal, "score", 0.0) or 0.0)
739	    band_scalar = _confidence_to_scalar(getattr(cal, "band", None))
740	    base_conf = max(cal_score, band_scalar * 0.5)
741	    pre_coverage = 1.0 - missing_pre.value if not missing_pre.low_confidence else 0.5
742	    grounded = base_conf * pre_coverage
743	    uncertainty = _clamp(1.0 - grounded + 0.5 * contradiction.value, 0.0, 1.0)
744	    return TransferMetric(
745	        name="outcome_uncertainty",
746	        value=uncertainty,
747	        range_low=0.0,
748	        range_high=1.0,
749	        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
750	        detail=(
751	            f"calibration_score={cal_score:.3f} band_scalar={band_scalar:.3f} "
752	            f"pre_coverage={pre_coverage:.3f}"
753	        ),
754	    )
755	
756	
757	def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
758	    if value.tzinfo is None:
759	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
760	    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
761	
762	
763	# ── Recommendation builder ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
764	
765	
766	def _decide_stance(
767	    fit: TransferMetric,
768	    missing: TransferMetric,
769	    contradiction: TransferMetric,
770	    case_distance: TransferMetric,
771	    domain_shift: TransferMetric,
772	    mechanism: TransferMetric,
773	    close_case_count: int,
774	) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
775	    reasons: list[str] = []
776	    if contradiction.value >= CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR and not contradiction.low_confidence:
777	        reasons.append(
778	            f"contradiction_risk={contradiction.value:.3f} ≥ {CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR}"
779	        )
780	        return TransferStance.DOES_NOT_APPLY, reasons
781	    if (
782	        missing.value >= MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR
783	        and not missing.low_confidence
784	    ):
785	        reasons.append(
786	            f"missing_preconditions={missing.value:.3f} ≥ {MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR}"
787	        )
788	        return TransferStance.ABSTAIN, reasons
789	    if fit.value < WATCH_FIT_FLOOR or mechanism.value < MECHANISM_MATCH_FLOOR:
790	        reasons.append(
791	            f"structural_fit={fit.value:.3f} mech_match={mechanism.value:.3f} below floor"
792	        )
793	        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
794	    if (
795	        case_distance.value >= CASE_DISTANCE_WATCH
796	        or close_case_count < MIN_CLOSE_CASES
797	    ):
798	        reasons.append(
799	            f"case_distance={case_distance.value:.3f} or close_cases={close_case_count} insufficient"
800	        )
801	        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
802	    if domain_shift.value >= DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH and not domain_shift.low_confidence:
803	        reasons.append(
804	            f"domain_shift={domain_shift.value:.3f} ≥ {DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH} — structural match in a different domain"
805	        )
806	        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
807	    if fit.value < APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR:
808	        reasons.append(
809	            f"structural_fit={fit.value:.3f} below APPLIES floor {APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR}"
810	        )
811	        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
812	    reasons.append(
813	        f"fit={fit.value:.3f} mech={mechanism.value:.3f} "
814	        f"contradiction={contradiction.value:.3f}"
815	    )
816	    return TransferStance.APPLIES, reasons
817	
818	
819	def _recommendation_confidence(
820	    fit: TransferMetric,
821	    mechanism: TransferMetric,
822	    case_distance: TransferMetric,
823	    contradiction: TransferMetric,
824	    outcome_uncertainty: TransferMetric,
825	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
826	) -> float:
827	    structural = (fit.value + mechanism.value + (1.0 - case_distance.value)) / 3.0
828	    risk_penalty = (contradiction.value + outcome_uncertainty.value) / 2.0
829	    calibration = float(getattr(principle.confidence, "score", 0.0) or 0.0)
830	    raw = max(0.0, structural - 0.5 * risk_penalty) * (0.5 + 0.5 * calibration)
831	    return _clamp(raw, 0.0, 1.0)
832	
833	
834	def _score_principle(
835	    query: TransferQuery,
836	    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
837	    *,
838	    cases_by_id: Mapping[str, EmpiricalCaseStudy],
839	    now: datetime,
840	    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None,
841	    reference_days: int,
842	) -> TransferRecommendation:
843	    distance, mechanism, closest_ids = _case_distance_and_mechanism(
844	        query, principle, cases_by_id
845	    )
846	    fit = _structural_fit(query, principle, mechanism_score=mechanism.value)
847	    missing = _missing_preconditions(query, principle)
848	    contradiction = _contradiction_risk(query, principle)
849	    shift = _domain_shift(query, principle)
850	    decay = _time_decay_risk(
851	        query,
852	        principle,
853	        now=now,
854	        case_dates=case_dates,
855	        reference_days=reference_days,
856	    )
857	    outcome = _outcome_uncertainty(principle, contradiction, missing)
858	
859	    # Close-case count: supporting cases whose structural similarity to
860	    # the query was above an explicit floor. We require at least two
861	    # such cases before APPLIES is reachable — a single match cannot
862	    # carry the stance.
863	    close_count = 0
864	    query_bag_text = _tokens(query.all_text())
865	    for cid in principle.supporting_case_ids:
866	        case = cases_by_id.get(cid)
867	        if case is None:
868	            continue
869	        if _overlap_coefficient(query_bag_text, _case_token_bag(case)) >= 0.15:
870	            close_count += 1
871	
872	    stance, reasons = _decide_stance(
873	        fit,
874	        missing,
875	        contradiction,
876	        distance,
877	        shift,
878	        mechanism,
879	        close_count,
880	    )
881	    confidence = _recommendation_confidence(fit, mechanism, distance, contradiction, outcome, principle)
882	
883	    metrics = (fit, missing, contradiction, distance, shift, decay, mechanism, outcome)
884	    return TransferRecommendation(
885	        principle_id=principle.id,
886	        canonical_statement=principle.canonical_statement,
887	        stance=stance,
888	        confidence=confidence,
889	        closest_case_ids=closest_ids,
890	        metrics=metrics,
891	        reasons=tuple(reasons),
892	    )
893	
894	
895	# ── Public engine ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
896	
897	
898	@dataclass(frozen=True)
899	class TransferEngineConfig:
900	    reference_decay_days: int = DEFAULT_TIME_DECAY_REFERENCE_DAYS
901	    top_k_recommendations: int = 5
902	
903	
904	def evaluate_transfer(
905	    *,
906	    query: TransferQuery,
907	    principles: Sequence[AbstractPrinciple] | TransferGraph,
908	    cases: Sequence[EmpiricalCaseStudy] = (),
909	    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None = None,
910	    now: datetime | None = None,
911	    config: TransferEngineConfig | None = None,
912	) -> TransferReport:
913	    """Score every candidate principle against ``query`` and assemble a report.
914	
915	    The engine never raises on missing data — principles without
916	    enough substrate yield ``WATCH`` or ``ABSTAIN`` recommendations
917	    with the relevant metrics flagged ``low_confidence``. Callers can
918	    therefore drop the report into a decision trace without defensive
919	    branches.
920	    """
921	
922	    cfg = config or TransferEngineConfig()
923	    now = _as_utc(now or datetime.now(timezone.utc))
924	
925	    if isinstance(principles, TransferGraph):
926	        principle_list = list(principles.principles)
927	    else:
928	        principle_list = list(principles)
929	
930	    cases_by_id = {c.id: c for c in cases}
931	
932	    recs: list[TransferRecommendation] = []
933	    for principle in principle_list:
934	        rec = _score_principle(
935	            query,
936	            principle,
937	            cases_by_id=cases_by_id,
938	            now=now,
939	            case_dates=case_dates,
940	            reference_days=cfg.reference_decay_days,
941	        )
942	        recs.append(rec)
943	
944	    # Rank: stance first, then confidence, then structural_fit.
945	    def _rank(rec: TransferRecommendation) -> tuple[int, float, float, str]:
946	        fit = rec.metric("structural_fit")
947	        return (
948	            -_STANCE_RANK.get(rec.stance, 0),
949	            -rec.confidence,
950	            -(fit.value if fit else 0.0),
951	            rec.principle_id,
952	        )
953	
954	    recs.sort(key=_rank)
955	    recs = recs[: cfg.top_k_recommendations] if cfg.top_k_recommendations > 0 else recs
956	
957	    if recs:
958	        best = recs[0]
959	        best_id = best.principle_id
960	        best_stance = best.stance
961	    else:
962	        best_id = None
963	        best_stance = TransferStance.ABSTAIN
964	
965	    summary = _aggregate_summary(recs)
966	
967	    return TransferReport(
968	        query_case_id=query.case_id,
969	        recommendations=tuple(recs),
970	        summary_metrics=summary,
971	        best_principle_id=best_id,
972	        best_stance=best_stance,
973	    )
974	
975	
976	def _aggregate_summary(
977	    recs: Sequence[TransferRecommendation],
978	) -> tuple[TransferMetric, ...]:
979	    """Aggregate per-recommendation metrics into report-level summaries.
980	
981	    For each metric name we surface the *maximum across applies-eligible
982	    recommendations* for fit/mechanism and the *minimum across
983	    applies-eligible recommendations* for risk metrics. This is what
984	    downstream consumers want — "across the principles we entertain,
985	    how well does the best one fit, and how risky is the least risky
986	    one?". When no recommendation reached APPLIES/WATCH we fall back
987	    to the global aggregate so the report is never empty.
988	    """
989	
990	    if not recs:
991	        return ()
992	
993	    eligible = [r for r in recs if r.stance in (TransferStance.APPLIES, TransferStance.WATCH)]
994	    pool = eligible or list(recs)
995	
996	    def _agg(name: str, mode: str) -> TransferMetric:
997	        values: list[float] = []
998	        lows: list[bool] = []
999	        details: list[str] = []
1000	        for rec in pool:
1001	            m = rec.metric(name)
1002	            if m is None:
1003	                continue
1004	            values.append(m.value)
1005	            lows.append(m.low_confidence)
1006	            details.append(f"{rec.principle_id}={m.value:.3f}")
1007	        if not values:
1008	            return TransferMetric(
1009	                name=f"summary_{name}",
1010	                value=0.0,
1011	                range_low=0.0,
1012	                range_high=1.0,
1013	                method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
1014	                low_confidence=True,
1015	                detail=f"no values for {name}",
1016	            )
1017	        value = max(values) if mode == "max" else min(values)
1018	        return TransferMetric(
1019	            name=f"summary_{name}",
1020	            value=_clamp(value, 0.0, 1.0),
1021	            range_low=0.0,
1022	            range_high=1.0,
1023	            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
1024	            low_confidence=all(lows),
1025	            detail="; ".join(details[:5]),
1026	        )
1027	
1028	    return (
1029	        _agg("structural_fit", "max"),
1030	        _agg("mechanism_match", "max"),
1031	        _agg("case_distance", "min"),
1032	        _agg("contradiction_risk", "min"),
1033	        _agg("missing_preconditions", "min"),
1034	        _agg("domain_shift", "min"),
1035	        _agg("time_decay_risk", "min"),
1036	        _agg("outcome_uncertainty", "min"),
1037	    )
1038	
1039	
1040	# ── Monitoring hooks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1041	#
1042	# These adapters convert upstream artifacts (Currents events, market
1043	# refreshes, uploads) into a :class:`TransferQuery`. They live here
1044	# instead of in the producing modules so the *transfer contract*
1045	# stays in one place — adding a new source means writing a single
1046	# adapter, not edits scattered across the producer.
1047	
1048	
1049	def query_from_currents_event(
1050	    event: Any,
1051	    *,
1052	    case_id: str | None = None,
1053	) -> TransferQuery:
1054	    """Build a TransferQuery from a Currents event row.
1055	
1056	    Accepts duck-typed objects (dataclasses, ORM rows, dicts) and
1057	    pulls the fields the engine cares about. Missing fields stay
1058	    empty rather than being faked.
1059	    """
1060	
1061	    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
1062	        if isinstance(event, Mapping):
1063	            return event.get(name, default)
1064	        return getattr(event, name, default)
1065	
1066	    return TransferQuery(
1067	        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or _get("event_id") or "currents_event"),
1068	        title=str(_get("title") or _get("headline") or "").strip(),
1069	        domain=str(_get("topic") or _get("category") or "").strip(),
1070	        actors=tuple(_get("actors", ()) or ()),
1071	        institutions=tuple(_get("institutions", ()) or ()),
1072	        mechanism=str(_get("mechanism", "")).strip(),
1073	        outcome_question=str(_get("question") or _get("outcome_question") or "").strip(),
1074	        source_text=str(_get("summary") or _get("body") or "").strip(),
1075	        observed_at=_get("observed_at") or _get("created_at") or None,
1076	    )
1077	
1078	
1079	def query_from_market(
1080	    market: Any,
1081	    *,
1082	    case_id: str | None = None,
1083	    extra_text: str = "",
1084	) -> TransferQuery:
1085	    """Build a TransferQuery from a ForecastMarket row (or duck-typed shim).
1086	
1087	    The adapter mirrors the recipe in
1088	    :func:`noosphere.forecasts.retrieval_adapter.build_query_from_market`
1089	    but emits the typed query shape transfer expects.
1090	    """
1091	
1092	    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
1093	        return getattr(market, name, default)
1094	
1095	    title = str(_get("title") or "").strip()
1096	    description = str(_get("description") or "").strip()
1097	    resolution = str(_get("resolution_criteria", _get("resolutionCriteria", ""))).strip()
1098	    category = str(_get("category") or "").strip()
1099	    return TransferQuery(
1100	        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or "market"),
1101	        title=title,
1102	        domain=category,
1103	        mechanism="",
1104	        outcome_question=title or resolution,
1105	        source_text="\n\n".join(p for p in (description, resolution, extra_text) if p),
1106	        observed_at=_get("updated_at") or _get("created_at") or None,
1107	    )
1108	
1109	
1110	def query_from_upload(
1111	    upload: Any,
1112	    *,
1113	    case_id: str | None = None,
1114	) -> TransferQuery:
1115	    """Build a TransferQuery from a Noosphere upload row.
1116	
1117	    The intent here is "we just ingested a new source — does it look
1118	    like a case that any existing principle would predict?". The
1119	    adapter pulls title/body/disciplines into the structural fields
1120	    so the engine can answer that without a separate extractor pass.
1121	    """
1122	
1123	    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
1124	        if isinstance(upload, Mapping):
1125	            return upload.get(name, default)
1126	        return getattr(upload, name, default)
1127	
1128	    disciplines = _get("disciplines", ()) or ()
1129	    if isinstance(disciplines, (str, bytes)):
1130	        disciplines = (str(disciplines),)
1131	    return TransferQuery(
1132	        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or "upload"),
1133	        title=str(_get("title") or "").strip(),
1134	        domain=str(disciplines[0]) if disciplines else "",
1135	        mechanism=str(_get("observed_mechanism", "")).strip(),
1136	        outcome_question=str(_get("outcome_question", "")).strip(),
1137	        source_text=str(_get("body") or _get("text") or "").strip(),
1138	        observed_at=_get("created_at") or None,
1139	    )
1140	
1141	
1142	__all__ = [
1143	    "APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR",
1144	    "CASE_DISTANCE_WATCH",
1145	    "CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR",
1146	    "DEFAULT_TIME_DECAY_REFERENCE_DAYS",
1147	    "DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH",
1148	    "MECHANISM_MATCH_FLOOR",
1149	    "MIN_CLOSE_CASES",
1150	    "MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR",
1151	    "TRANSFER_VERSION",
1152	    "TransferEngineConfig",
1153	    "TransferMetric",
1154	    "TransferQuery",
1155	    "TransferRecommendation",
1156	    "TransferReport",
1157	    "TransferStance",
1158	    "WATCH_FIT_FLOOR",
1159	    "evaluate_transfer",
1160	    "query_from_currents_event",
1161	    "query_from_market",
1162	    "query_from_upload",
1163	]
1164

file.content:
"""Analogical transfer engine — apply abstract principles to a new situation.

Given a new event/market/case, this module asks: which of our recorded
:class:`AbstractPrinciple` instances plausibly apply, which of their
preconditions are satisfied, which failure conditions are tripped, and
which prior cases are *structurally* analogous (not merely
keyword-similar). The output is a typed :class:`TransferReport` whose
:class:`TransferRecommendation` rows are reproducible from the inputs
alone — no LLM calls, no randomness — so a forecast decision trace can
quote them by id.

What the engine deliberately does *not* do:

- **Promote analogy to proof.** A recommendation with stance
  ``APPLIES`` is a hypothesis with a defensible bracket, never a
  conclusion. Downstream callers (e.g. :mod:`noosphere.forecasts.
  decision_metrics`) consume the report as one input among several;
  they do not let any single recommendation force a decision past
  ``PAPER_TRADE`` unless the rule graph already had grounds.
- **Cluster by keyword.** Token Jaccard is one of several axes; we
  separately score precondition coverage, mechanism match, and
  failure-signal presence. A passage that *says* "regulatory capture"
  without describing the mechanism scores poorly on mechanism_match
  even if it shares vocabulary with a hundred prior cases.
- **Let a single case dominate.** The aggregator weighs the *median*
  closest case alongside the single closest, and a recommendation's
  confidence is pulled down whenever fewer than two supporting cases
  reach a structural-fit floor.

The metrics catalog is intentionally aligned with — but distinct from —
``noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics``. Decision metrics are about
the *market* (edge, liquidity, calibration); transfer metrics are
about the *principle's applicability to the new situation*. Both feed
the same trace.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from statistics import median
from typing import Any, Iterable, Mapping, Sequence

from noosphere.cases.models import EmpiricalCaseStudy
from noosphere.principles.models import (
    AbstractPrinciple,
    PrincipleConfidence,
    PrincipleStatus,
    TransferGraph,
)


TRANSFER_VERSION = "analogical_transfer@v1"


# Thresholds. Tuned to be conservative: a principle has to *clearly*
# fit before it earns ``APPLIES``, and a single contradiction signal is
# enough to drop it to ``DOES_NOT_APPLY``.
APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR = 0.45
WATCH_FIT_FLOOR = 0.20
MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR = 0.50
CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR = 0.50
CASE_DISTANCE_WATCH = 0.80
DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH = 0.70
MECHANISM_MATCH_FLOOR = 0.10
MIN_CLOSE_CASES = 2
DEFAULT_TIME_DECAY_REFERENCE_DAYS = 18 * 31


# A small stop list for token comparison. Without this, "the", "with",
# "for", and other ≥3-char function words inflate Jaccard scores so
# that superficially similar text scores higher than structurally
# similar text. The list is deliberately tiny — domain-specific
# stopwording belongs upstream.
_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
    {
        "the",
        "and",
        "for",
        "with",
        "from",
        "that",
        "this",
        "are",
        "was",
        "were",
        "has",
        "have",
        "had",
        "will",
        "would",
        "could",
        "should",
        "into",
        "onto",
        "over",
        "under",
        "than",
        "then",
        "but",
        "not",
        "any",
        "all",
        "some",
        "such",
        "their",
        "there",
        "where",
        "when",
        "what",
        "which",
        "while",
        "about",
        "between",
        "across",
        "after",
        "before",
        "because",
        "been",
        "being",
        "they",
        "them",
        "its",
        "his",
        "her",
        "our",
        "your",
    }
)


def _tokens(value: Any) -> set[str]:
    if value is None:
        return set()
    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple, set, frozenset)):
        out: set[str] = set()
        for item in value:
            out |= _tokens(item)
        return out
    text = str(value)
    pieces = "".join(ch.lower() if ch.isalnum() else " " for ch in text).split()
    return {token for token in pieces if len(token) >= 3 and token not in _STOPWORDS}


def _jaccard(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
    if not left or not right:
        return 0.0
    return len(left & right) / len(left | right)


def _overlap_coefficient(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
    """Symmetric overlap normalized by the smaller set.

    Jaccard punishes large token bags that share a meaningful core,
    which is the wrong signal when comparing case-token bags (rich on
    one side, sparse on the other). Overlap coefficient asks
    "how much of the smaller bag is shared?", which is closer to what
    structural similarity means here. Used for case-bag-against-bag
    comparison; precondition / mechanism comparisons keep using
    Jaccard since both sides are small and targeted.
    """

    if not left or not right:
        return 0.0
    return len(left & right) / min(len(left), len(right))


def _clamp(value: float, low: float, high: float) -> float:
    return max(low, min(high, value))


def _confidence_to_scalar(band: Any) -> float:
    name = getattr(band, "value", band)
    if name == PrincipleConfidence.HIGH.value:
        return 1.0
    if name == PrincipleConfidence.MODERATE.value:
        return 0.65
    return 0.35


# ── Inputs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TransferQuery:
    """Description of a new situation against which principles are tested.

    Only ``case_id`` and a usable text (any of ``title``, ``mechanism``,
    ``outcome_question``, ``source_text``) are required. Empty fields
    are treated as missing, not falsified — the engine reports
    ``low_confidence`` rather than scoring against absent data.
    """

    case_id: str
    title: str = ""
    domain: str = ""
    actors: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    institutions: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    mechanism: str = ""
    preconditions_present: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    preconditions_absent: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    failure_signals_present: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    outcome_question: str = ""
    time_horizon_days: int | None = None
    source_text: str = ""
    observed_at: datetime | None = None

    def all_text(self) -> str:
        parts = [
            self.title,
            self.mechanism,
            self.outcome_question,
            self.source_text,
            " ".join(self.actors),
            " ".join(self.institutions),
            " ".join(self.preconditions_present),
            " ".join(self.failure_signals_present),
        ]
        return " ".join(p for p in parts if p)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TransferMetric:
    name: str
    value: float
    range_low: float
    range_high: float
    method: str
    low_confidence: bool = False
    detail: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "value": round(float(self.value), 6),
            "range": [self.range_low, self.range_high],
            "method": self.method,
            "low_confidence": bool(self.low_confidence),
            "detail": self.detail,
        }


class TransferStance(str):
    """String constants for stance values.

    Kept as plain strings (not :class:`Enum`) so a recommendation
    serializes byte-identically across processes without enum
    representation drift.
    """

    APPLIES = "APPLIES"
    WATCH = "WATCH"
    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
    DOES_NOT_APPLY = "DOES_NOT_APPLY"


_STANCE_RANK = {
    TransferStance.DOES_NOT_APPLY: 0,
    TransferStance.ABSTAIN: 1,
    TransferStance.WATCH: 2,
    TransferStance.APPLIES: 3,
}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TransferRecommendation:
    principle_id: str
    canonical_statement: str
    stance: str
    confidence: float
    closest_case_ids: tuple[str, ...]
    metrics: tuple[TransferMetric, ...]
    reasons: tuple[str, ...]

    def metric(self, name: str) -> TransferMetric | None:
        for m in self.metrics:
            if m.name == name:
                return m
        return None

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "principle_id": self.principle_id,
            "canonical_statement": self.canonical_statement,
            "stance": self.stance,
            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
            "closest_case_ids": list(self.closest_case_ids),
            "metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.metrics],
            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TransferReport:
    query_case_id: str
    recommendations: tuple[TransferRecommendation, ...]
    summary_metrics: tuple[TransferMetric, ...]
    best_principle_id: str | None
    best_stance: str
    trace_version: str = TRANSFER_VERSION

    def recommendation(self, principle_id: str) -> TransferRecommendation | None:
        for rec in self.recommendations:
            if rec.principle_id == principle_id:
                return rec
        return None

    def summary(self, name: str) -> TransferMetric | None:
        for m in self.summary_metrics:
            if m.name == name:
                return m
        return None

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "query_case_id": self.query_case_id,
            "best_principle_id": self.best_principle_id,
            "best_stance": self.best_stance,
            "recommendations": [r.to_dict() for r in self.recommendations],
            "summary_metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.summary_metrics],
            "trace_version": self.trace_version,
        }


# ── Per-pair scoring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _structural_fit(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
    *,
    mechanism_score: float,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """Composite of precondition coverage, mechanism match (computed
    upstream against supporting cases), and outcome alignment.

    Preconditions and outcomes are scored with Jaccard against the
    principle's own targeted fields; mechanism is supplied by the
    caller so the same case-aware mechanism scoring used elsewhere
    in the report is what feeds the fit axis. This avoids penalizing
    a principle whose terse one-line ``mechanism`` text happens not
    to share vocabulary with the query when the supporting cases'
    mechanisms clearly do.
    """

    query_text = _tokens(query.all_text())
    pre_terms = _tokens(principle.preconditions)
    out_terms = _tokens(principle.expected_outcomes)

    pre_score = _jaccard(_tokens(query.preconditions_present), pre_terms) if pre_terms else 0.0
    if pre_terms and not query.preconditions_present:
        pre_score = max(pre_score, _jaccard(query_text, pre_terms) * 0.5)
    out_score = (
        _overlap_coefficient(_tokens(query.outcome_question) or query_text, out_terms)
        if out_terms
        else 0.0
    )

    components = (pre_score, mechanism_score, out_score)
    if all(c == 0.0 for c in components):
        return TransferMetric(
            name="structural_fit",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no structural overlap on preconditions, mechanism, or outcome",
        )

    weights = (0.45, 0.4, 0.15)
    weighted = sum(w * v for w, v in zip(weights, components))
    return TransferMetric(
        name="structural_fit",
        value=_clamp(weighted, 0.0, 1.0),
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=(
            f"pre={pre_score:.3f} mech={mechanism_score:.3f} outcome={out_score:.3f}"
        ),
    )


def _missing_preconditions(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """Fraction of recorded preconditions whose tokens are *not* present
    in the query's stated preconditions or surrounding text.
    """

    if not principle.preconditions:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="missing_preconditions",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="principle has no stated preconditions",
        )
    haystack = _tokens(query.preconditions_present) | _tokens(query.all_text())
    missing = 0
    for pre in principle.preconditions:
        terms = _tokens(pre)
        if not terms:
            continue
        if not (terms & haystack):
            missing += 1
    fraction = missing / len(principle.preconditions)
    return TransferMetric(
        name="missing_preconditions",
        value=_clamp(fraction, 0.0, 1.0),
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=f"{missing}/{len(principle.preconditions)} preconditions unmatched",
    )


def _contradiction_risk(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """Fraction of failure conditions whose detectable signal appears
    in the query, plus a boost when a negation candidate's tokens are
    densely present in the query text.
    """

    fc = principle.failure_conditions
    nc = principle.negation_candidates
    if not fc and not nc:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="contradiction_risk",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="principle has no failure_conditions or negation_candidates",
        )

    # Baseline tokens describe the *principle itself*; they appear in
    # failure-condition descriptions only as context. We strip them so
    # the test asks "are the failure-specific tokens present?" rather
    # than "does the failure description share any vocabulary with
    # the query?".
    baseline = (
        _tokens(principle.preconditions)
        | _tokens(principle.mechanism)
        | _tokens(principle.expected_outcomes)
        | _tokens(principle.domain)
    )
    explicit_signals = _tokens(query.failure_signals_present) | _tokens(
        query.preconditions_absent
    )
    text_signals = _tokens(query.all_text())
    fc_hits = 0
    for cond in fc:
        signal_terms = _tokens(cond.detectable_signal) - baseline
        desc_terms = _tokens(cond.description) - baseline
        # An explicit failure signal is a strong contradiction: even
        # a single specific match counts. Inferring contradiction from
        # ambient text requires a denser overlap so a stray word in
        # a long passage doesn't trigger the veto.
        if signal_terms and (signal_terms & explicit_signals):
            fc_hits += 1
            continue
        novel = signal_terms | desc_terms
        if not novel:
            continue
        overlap = novel & text_signals
        if len(overlap) >= 2 or (overlap and len(novel) <= 3):
            fc_hits += 1
    fc_fraction = (fc_hits / len(fc)) if fc else 0.0

    nc_score = 0.0
    if nc:
        nc_terms: set[str] = set()
        for n in nc:
            nc_terms |= _tokens(n.statement)
        nc_specific = nc_terms - baseline
        if nc_specific:
            nc_score = _jaccard(nc_specific, text_signals)

    value = _clamp(max(fc_fraction, nc_score), 0.0, 1.0)
    return TransferMetric(
        name="contradiction_risk",
        value=value,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=(
            f"failure_hits={fc_hits}/{len(fc) if fc else 0} "
            f"negation_jaccard={nc_score:.3f}"
        ),
    )


def _case_token_bag(case: EmpiricalCaseStudy) -> set[str]:
    return (
        _tokens(case.observed_mechanism)
        | _tokens(case.outcome)
        | _tokens(case.actors)
        | _tokens(case.institutions)
        | _tokens(case.stated_causal_claim)
        | _tokens(case.title)
        | _tokens(case.domain)
    )


def _case_distance_and_mechanism(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
    cases_by_id: Mapping[str, EmpiricalCaseStudy],
) -> tuple[TransferMetric, TransferMetric, tuple[str, ...]]:
    """Closest-case distance + mechanism match, plus the case ids that
    contributed to the closest scores.

    Returns three things in one pass to avoid re-walking the supporting
    case list.
    """

    supporting_ids = list(principle.supporting_case_ids)
    bounding_ids = list(principle.bounding_case_ids)
    contradicting_ids = list(principle.contradicting_case_ids)
    relevant_ids = supporting_ids + bounding_ids + contradicting_ids

    query_bag = _tokens(query.all_text())
    query_mech = _tokens(query.mechanism) or query_bag

    similarities: list[tuple[str, float, float]] = []  # (case_id, structural_sim, mech_sim)
    for cid in relevant_ids:
        case = cases_by_id.get(cid)
        if case is None:
            continue
        case_bag = _case_token_bag(case)
        structural = _overlap_coefficient(query_bag, case_bag)
        mech_sim = _overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(case.observed_mechanism))
        similarities.append((cid, structural, mech_sim))

    if not similarities:
        distance = TransferMetric(
            name="case_distance",
            value=1.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no supporting / bounding / contradicting cases available",
        )
        mechanism = TransferMetric(
            name="mechanism_match",
            value=_overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(principle.mechanism)),
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="no case-level mechanism evidence; using principle.mechanism",
        )
        return distance, mechanism, ()

    similarities.sort(key=lambda triple: triple[1], reverse=True)
    structural_scores = [s for _, s, _ in similarities]
    mech_scores = [m for _, _, m in similarities]
    closest_struct = structural_scores[0]
    median_struct = median(structural_scores)
    closest_ids = tuple(cid for cid, s, _ in similarities[: min(3, len(similarities))])

    distance_value = _clamp(1.0 - ((closest_struct + median_struct) / 2.0), 0.0, 1.0)
    distance = TransferMetric(
        name="case_distance",
        value=distance_value,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=f"closest={closest_struct:.3f} median={median_struct:.3f} n={len(similarities)}",
    )

    mech_principle = _overlap_coefficient(query_mech, _tokens(principle.mechanism))
    mech_value = _clamp(max(max(mech_scores), mech_principle), 0.0, 1.0)
    mechanism = TransferMetric(
        name="mechanism_match",
        value=mech_value,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=(
            f"max_case_mech={max(mech_scores):.3f} principle_mech={mech_principle:.3f}"
        ),
    )
    return distance, mechanism, closest_ids


def _domain_shift(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """0 if the query domain matches the principle's domain or scope;
    1 if no overlap; intermediate if the principle has broad scope but
    no exact match.
    """

    q_domain = (query.domain or "").strip().lower()
    p_domain = (principle.domain or "").strip().lower()
    scope_terms = {s.strip().lower() for s in principle.scope if s}

    if not q_domain and not p_domain and not scope_terms:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="domain_shift",
            value=0.5,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="neither query nor principle declares a domain",
        )
    if not q_domain:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="domain_shift",
            value=0.6,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=True,
            detail="query has no declared domain",
        )

    if q_domain == p_domain or q_domain in scope_terms:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="domain_shift",
            value=0.0,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            detail=f"query domain {q_domain!r} matches principle scope",
        )
    if any(q_domain in s or s in q_domain for s in {p_domain} | scope_terms if s):
        return TransferMetric(
            name="domain_shift",
            value=0.4,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            detail=f"partial domain overlap with scope={sorted(scope_terms)}",
        )
    return TransferMetric(
        name="domain_shift",
        value=1.0,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=(
            f"query domain {q_domain!r} not in principle.domain={p_domain!r} "
            f"or scope={sorted(scope_terms)}"
        ),
    )


def _time_decay_risk(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
    *,
    now: datetime,
    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None,
    reference_days: int,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """Older supporting cases → higher decay risk. ``case_dates`` is
    an optional caller-supplied map of case_id → observation date; when
    absent we fall back to the principle's status (a ``BOUNDED`` or
    ``CONTRADICTED`` principle implies the recorded scope has already
    drifted, which is itself a decay signal).
    """

    supporting = list(principle.supporting_case_ids)
    if case_dates and supporting:
        ages = []
        for cid in supporting:
            date = case_dates.get(cid)
            if date is None:
                continue
            ages.append(max(0.0, (now - _as_utc(date)).total_seconds() / 86400.0))
        if ages:
            mean_age = sum(ages) / len(ages)
            value = _clamp(mean_age / max(1.0, float(reference_days)), 0.0, 1.0)
            return TransferMetric(
                name="time_decay_risk",
                value=value,
                range_low=0.0,
                range_high=1.0,
                method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
                detail=f"mean_supporting_age_days={mean_age:.1f} ref={reference_days}d",
            )

    status = getattr(principle.status, "value", principle.status)
    if status == PrincipleStatus.CONTRADICTED.value:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="time_decay_risk",
            value=0.8,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            detail="principle status=CONTRADICTED",
        )
    if status == PrincipleStatus.BOUNDED.value:
        return TransferMetric(
            name="time_decay_risk",
            value=0.5,
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            detail="principle status=BOUNDED — scope drift recorded",
        )
    return TransferMetric(
        name="time_decay_risk",
        value=0.3,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        low_confidence=True,
        detail="no case dates supplied; using neutral prior",
    )


def _outcome_uncertainty(
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
    contradiction: TransferMetric,
    missing_pre: TransferMetric,
) -> TransferMetric:
    """1 − (principle calibration × precondition coverage), bumped by
    contradiction risk. The calibration is the principle's recorded
    confidence band — it caps how confident *any* transfer recommendation
    can be even when the structural axes align.
    """

    cal = principle.confidence
    cal_score = float(getattr(cal, "score", 0.0) or 0.0)
    band_scalar = _confidence_to_scalar(getattr(cal, "band", None))
    base_conf = max(cal_score, band_scalar * 0.5)
    pre_coverage = 1.0 - missing_pre.value if not missing_pre.low_confidence else 0.5
    grounded = base_conf * pre_coverage
    uncertainty = _clamp(1.0 - grounded + 0.5 * contradiction.value, 0.0, 1.0)
    return TransferMetric(
        name="outcome_uncertainty",
        value=uncertainty,
        range_low=0.0,
        range_high=1.0,
        method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
        detail=(
            f"calibration_score={cal_score:.3f} band_scalar={band_scalar:.3f} "
            f"pre_coverage={pre_coverage:.3f}"
        ),
    )


def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
    if value.tzinfo is None:
        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)


# ── Recommendation builder ──────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _decide_stance(
    fit: TransferMetric,
    missing: TransferMetric,
    contradiction: TransferMetric,
    case_distance: TransferMetric,
    domain_shift: TransferMetric,
    mechanism: TransferMetric,
    close_case_count: int,
) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    reasons: list[str] = []
    if contradiction.value >= CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR and not contradiction.low_confidence:
        reasons.append(
            f"contradiction_risk={contradiction.value:.3f} ≥ {CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR}"
        )
        return TransferStance.DOES_NOT_APPLY, reasons
    if (
        missing.value >= MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR
        and not missing.low_confidence
    ):
        reasons.append(
            f"missing_preconditions={missing.value:.3f} ≥ {MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR}"
        )
        return TransferStance.ABSTAIN, reasons
    if fit.value < WATCH_FIT_FLOOR or mechanism.value < MECHANISM_MATCH_FLOOR:
        reasons.append(
            f"structural_fit={fit.value:.3f} mech_match={mechanism.value:.3f} below floor"
        )
        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
    if (
        case_distance.value >= CASE_DISTANCE_WATCH
        or close_case_count < MIN_CLOSE_CASES
    ):
        reasons.append(
            f"case_distance={case_distance.value:.3f} or close_cases={close_case_count} insufficient"
        )
        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
    if domain_shift.value >= DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH and not domain_shift.low_confidence:
        reasons.append(
            f"domain_shift={domain_shift.value:.3f} ≥ {DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH} — structural match in a different domain"
        )
        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
    if fit.value < APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR:
        reasons.append(
            f"structural_fit={fit.value:.3f} below APPLIES floor {APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR}"
        )
        return TransferStance.WATCH, reasons
    reasons.append(
        f"fit={fit.value:.3f} mech={mechanism.value:.3f} "
        f"contradiction={contradiction.value:.3f}"
    )
    return TransferStance.APPLIES, reasons


def _recommendation_confidence(
    fit: TransferMetric,
    mechanism: TransferMetric,
    case_distance: TransferMetric,
    contradiction: TransferMetric,
    outcome_uncertainty: TransferMetric,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
) -> float:
    structural = (fit.value + mechanism.value + (1.0 - case_distance.value)) / 3.0
    risk_penalty = (contradiction.value + outcome_uncertainty.value) / 2.0
    calibration = float(getattr(principle.confidence, "score", 0.0) or 0.0)
    raw = max(0.0, structural - 0.5 * risk_penalty) * (0.5 + 0.5 * calibration)
    return _clamp(raw, 0.0, 1.0)


def _score_principle(
    query: TransferQuery,
    principle: AbstractPrinciple,
    *,
    cases_by_id: Mapping[str, EmpiricalCaseStudy],
    now: datetime,
    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None,
    reference_days: int,
) -> TransferRecommendation:
    distance, mechanism, closest_ids = _case_distance_and_mechanism(
        query, principle, cases_by_id
    )
    fit = _structural_fit(query, principle, mechanism_score=mechanism.value)
    missing = _missing_preconditions(query, principle)
    contradiction = _contradiction_risk(query, principle)
    shift = _domain_shift(query, principle)
    decay = _time_decay_risk(
        query,
        principle,
        now=now,
        case_dates=case_dates,
        reference_days=reference_days,
    )
    outcome = _outcome_uncertainty(principle, contradiction, missing)

    # Close-case count: supporting cases whose structural similarity to
    # the query was above an explicit floor. We require at least two
    # such cases before APPLIES is reachable — a single match cannot
    # carry the stance.
    close_count = 0
    query_bag_text = _tokens(query.all_text())
    for cid in principle.supporting_case_ids:
        case = cases_by_id.get(cid)
        if case is None:
            continue
        if _overlap_coefficient(query_bag_text, _case_token_bag(case)) >= 0.15:
            close_count += 1

    stance, reasons = _decide_stance(
        fit,
        missing,
        contradiction,
        distance,
        shift,
        mechanism,
        close_count,
    )
    confidence = _recommendation_confidence(fit, mechanism, distance, contradiction, outcome, principle)

    metrics = (fit, missing, contradiction, distance, shift, decay, mechanism, outcome)
    return TransferRecommendation(
        principle_id=principle.id,
        canonical_statement=principle.canonical_statement,
        stance=stance,
        confidence=confidence,
        closest_case_ids=closest_ids,
        metrics=metrics,
        reasons=tuple(reasons),
    )


# ── Public engine ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TransferEngineConfig:
    reference_decay_days: int = DEFAULT_TIME_DECAY_REFERENCE_DAYS
    top_k_recommendations: int = 5


def evaluate_transfer(
    *,
    query: TransferQuery,
    principles: Sequence[AbstractPrinciple] | TransferGraph,
    cases: Sequence[EmpiricalCaseStudy] = (),
    case_dates: Mapping[str, datetime] | None = None,
    now: datetime | None = None,
    config: TransferEngineConfig | None = None,
) -> TransferReport:
    """Score every candidate principle against ``query`` and assemble a report.

    The engine never raises on missing data — principles without
    enough substrate yield ``WATCH`` or ``ABSTAIN`` recommendations
    with the relevant metrics flagged ``low_confidence``. Callers can
    therefore drop the report into a decision trace without defensive
    branches.
    """

    cfg = config or TransferEngineConfig()
    now = _as_utc(now or datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    if isinstance(principles, TransferGraph):
        principle_list = list(principles.principles)
    else:
        principle_list = list(principles)

    cases_by_id = {c.id: c for c in cases}

    recs: list[TransferRecommendation] = []
    for principle in principle_list:
        rec = _score_principle(
            query,
            principle,
            cases_by_id=cases_by_id,
            now=now,
            case_dates=case_dates,
            reference_days=cfg.reference_decay_days,
        )
        recs.append(rec)

    # Rank: stance first, then confidence, then structural_fit.
    def _rank(rec: TransferRecommendation) -> tuple[int, float, float, str]:
        fit = rec.metric("structural_fit")
        return (
            -_STANCE_RANK.get(rec.stance, 0),
            -rec.confidence,
            -(fit.value if fit else 0.0),
            rec.principle_id,
        )

    recs.sort(key=_rank)
    recs = recs[: cfg.top_k_recommendations] if cfg.top_k_recommendations > 0 else recs

    if recs:
        best = recs[0]
        best_id = best.principle_id
        best_stance = best.stance
    else:
        best_id = None
        best_stance = TransferStance.ABSTAIN

    summary = _aggregate_summary(recs)

    return TransferReport(
        query_case_id=query.case_id,
        recommendations=tuple(recs),
        summary_metrics=summary,
        best_principle_id=best_id,
        best_stance=best_stance,
    )


def _aggregate_summary(
    recs: Sequence[TransferRecommendation],
) -> tuple[TransferMetric, ...]:
    """Aggregate per-recommendation metrics into report-level summaries.

    For each metric name we surface the *maximum across applies-eligible
    recommendations* for fit/mechanism and the *minimum across
    applies-eligible recommendations* for risk metrics. This is what
    downstream consumers want — "across the principles we entertain,
    how well does the best one fit, and how risky is the least risky
    one?". When no recommendation reached APPLIES/WATCH we fall back
    to the global aggregate so the report is never empty.
    """

    if not recs:
        return ()

    eligible = [r for r in recs if r.stance in (TransferStance.APPLIES, TransferStance.WATCH)]
    pool = eligible or list(recs)

    def _agg(name: str, mode: str) -> TransferMetric:
        values: list[float] = []
        lows: list[bool] = []
        details: list[str] = []
        for rec in pool:
            m = rec.metric(name)
            if m is None:
                continue
            values.append(m.value)
            lows.append(m.low_confidence)
            details.append(f"{rec.principle_id}={m.value:.3f}")
        if not values:
            return TransferMetric(
                name=f"summary_{name}",
                value=0.0,
                range_low=0.0,
                range_high=1.0,
                method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
                low_confidence=True,
                detail=f"no values for {name}",
            )
        value = max(values) if mode == "max" else min(values)
        return TransferMetric(
            name=f"summary_{name}",
            value=_clamp(value, 0.0, 1.0),
            range_low=0.0,
            range_high=1.0,
            method=TRANSFER_VERSION,
            low_confidence=all(lows),
            detail="; ".join(details[:5]),
        )

    return (
        _agg("structural_fit", "max"),
        _agg("mechanism_match", "max"),
        _agg("case_distance", "min"),
        _agg("contradiction_risk", "min"),
        _agg("missing_preconditions", "min"),
        _agg("domain_shift", "min"),
        _agg("time_decay_risk", "min"),
        _agg("outcome_uncertainty", "min"),
    )


# ── Monitoring hooks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# These adapters convert upstream artifacts (Currents events, market
# refreshes, uploads) into a :class:`TransferQuery`. They live here
# instead of in the producing modules so the *transfer contract*
# stays in one place — adding a new source means writing a single
# adapter, not edits scattered across the producer.


def query_from_currents_event(
    event: Any,
    *,
    case_id: str | None = None,
) -> TransferQuery:
    """Build a TransferQuery from a Currents event row.

    Accepts duck-typed objects (dataclasses, ORM rows, dicts) and
    pulls the fields the engine cares about. Missing fields stay
    empty rather than being faked.
    """

    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
        if isinstance(event, Mapping):
            return event.get(name, default)
        return getattr(event, name, default)

    return TransferQuery(
        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or _get("event_id") or "currents_event"),
        title=str(_get("title") or _get("headline") or "").strip(),
        domain=str(_get("topic") or _get("category") or "").strip(),
        actors=tuple(_get("actors", ()) or ()),
        institutions=tuple(_get("institutions", ()) or ()),
        mechanism=str(_get("mechanism", "")).strip(),
        outcome_question=str(_get("question") or _get("outcome_question") or "").strip(),
        source_text=str(_get("summary") or _get("body") or "").strip(),
        observed_at=_get("observed_at") or _get("created_at") or None,
    )


def query_from_market(
    market: Any,
    *,
    case_id: str | None = None,
    extra_text: str = "",
) -> TransferQuery:
    """Build a TransferQuery from a ForecastMarket row (or duck-typed shim).

    The adapter mirrors the recipe in
    :func:`noosphere.forecasts.retrieval_adapter.build_query_from_market`
    but emits the typed query shape transfer expects.
    """

    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
        return getattr(market, name, default)

    title = str(_get("title") or "").strip()
    description = str(_get("description") or "").strip()
    resolution = str(_get("resolution_criteria", _get("resolutionCriteria", ""))).strip()
    category = str(_get("category") or "").strip()
    return TransferQuery(
        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or "market"),
        title=title,
        domain=category,
        mechanism="",
        outcome_question=title or resolution,
        source_text="\n\n".join(p for p in (description, resolution, extra_text) if p),
        observed_at=_get("updated_at") or _get("created_at") or None,
    )


def query_from_upload(
    upload: Any,
    *,
    case_id: str | None = None,
) -> TransferQuery:
    """Build a TransferQuery from a Noosphere upload row.

    The intent here is "we just ingested a new source — does it look
    like a case that any existing principle would predict?". The
    adapter pulls title/body/disciplines into the structural fields
    so the engine can answer that without a separate extractor pass.
    """

    def _get(name: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
        if isinstance(upload, Mapping):
            return upload.get(name, default)
        return getattr(upload, name, default)

    disciplines = _get("disciplines", ()) or ()
    if isinstance(disciplines, (str, bytes)):
        disciplines = (str(disciplines),)
    return TransferQuery(
        case_id=str(case_id or _get("id") or "upload"),
        title=str(_get("title") or "").strip(),
        domain=str(disciplines[0]) if disciplines else "",
        mechanism=str(_get("observed_mechanism", "")).strip(),
        outcome_question=str(_get("outcome_question", "")).strip(),
        source_text=str(_get("body") or _get("text") or "").strip(),
        observed_at=_get("created_at") or None,
    )


__all__ = [
    "APPLIES_FIT_FLOOR",
    "CASE_DISTANCE_WATCH",
    "CONTRADICTION_DROP_FLOOR",
    "DEFAULT_TIME_DECAY_REFERENCE_DAYS",
    "DOMAIN_SHIFT_WATCH",
    "MECHANISM_MATCH_FLOOR",
    "MIN_CLOSE_CASES",
    "MISSING_PRECONDITION_FLOOR",
    "TRANSFER_VERSION",
    "TransferEngineConfig",
    "TransferMetric",
    "TransferQuery",
    "TransferRecommendation",
    "TransferReport",
    "TransferStance",
    "WATCH_FIT_FLOOR",
    "evaluate_transfer",
    "query_from_currents_event",
    "query_from_market",
    "query_from_upload",
]
